inputs.userGoal: Configure an autoscaled VMSS-based presentation tier, application servers in separate subnet, and encrypted SQL Managed Instance backend database

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides network isolation across presentation, application, and data tiers for security and operational separation.
reasoning.tradeoffs: More network configuration complexity in exchange for stronger segmentation.

resources.name: subnetPresentation

reasoning.whyChosen: Dedicated subnet for internet-facing tier limits blast radius and supports separate NSG controls.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Uses more IP space but improves security boundaries.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Subnet is scoped to the virtual network.

resources.name: subnetApplication

reasoning.whyChosen: Keeps app servers isolated from presentation nodes and backend data services.
alternativesConsidered: Application servers in same subnet as presentation tier

resources.dependencyReasoning: Subnet is scoped to the virtual network.

resources.name: subnetManagedInstance

reasoning.whyChosen: SQL Managed Instance requires a dedicated delegated subnet to satisfy platform networking requirements.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Managed Instance has higher baseline cost but better SQL Server compatibility.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Managed Instance subnet must be created within the VNet before SQL MI deployment.

resources.name: lbPresentation

reasoning.whyChosen: Distributes inbound traffic to presentation VMSS instances and provides high availability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Layer-4 balancing is simpler but lacks Layer-7 routing and WAF features.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Presentation tier endpoints are in the presentation subnet.

resources.name: vmssPresentation

reasoning.whyChosen: VMSS provides elastic scale for the presentation tier with built-in high availability and zone support.
reasoning.tradeoffs: More OS management overhead than PaaS but greater VM-level control.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Presentation instances require subnet placement and load balancer backend association.

resources.name: autoscalePresentation

reasoning.whyChosen: Externalized autoscale profile keeps scaling policy explicit and adjustable without VMSS image changes.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Requires monitoring configuration and tuning thresholds over time.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Autoscale setting targets the VMSS resource.

resources.name: vmssApplication

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides multiple application servers in a dedicated subnet while preserving consistency and simple horizontal growth.
reasoning.tradeoffs: VMSS operational model may be more than needed for static capacity.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Application tier nodes are deployed only into the application subnet.

resources.name: sqlManagedInstance001

reasoning.whyChosen: Managed Instance offers near-full SQL Server compatibility with built-in TDE encryption and private VNet deployment.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Higher fixed cost and longer provisioning time than single database offerings.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Managed Instance requires the delegated dedicated subnet before deployment.

overallReasoning.summary: A three-tier network-segmented architecture is selected: autoscaled presentation VMSS behind a public load balancer, isolated application server pool in a separate subnet, and private encrypted SQL Managed Instance in a dedicated delegated subnet.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: This design prioritizes compatibility, isolation, and elasticity, at the cost of higher baseline compute/database spend and greater network configuration complexity.

plan.validation: All compute tiers and SQL MI are colocated in eastus; dependencies enforce VNet/subnet-first deployment and autoscale target binding for VMSS.
